name: Percy Humphrey
abstract: Percy Gaston Humphrey (January 13, 1905 – July 22, 1995) was an American jazz trumpeter and bandleader in New Orleans, Louisiana. In addition to his band, Percy Humphrey and His Crescent City Joymakers, for more than thirty years he was leader of the Eureka Brass Band.

interviews.title: Percy Humphrey Band: Percy Humphrey, Jim Robinson, Ernest Roubleau, Josiah Frazier Interview, 1972-02-29

about_quotes.speaker: Louis James
about_quotes.evidence: I played at the country club with Percy. ... Well I played with his band ... You say you played with Percy Humphrey then a while too? Yeah, yeah. I used to play up at Dastry and Percy Humphrey.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Watkins
about_quotes.evidence: That was the first time I ever played with him. ... I worked with him many times. Because he played with us back at Manners.

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: There were a few who was really trying to learn to play jazz and some was pretty good at it already. And they were usually older than me. Now there were the traditional guys who had been playing trad for a long time, like the Humphrey brothers, Percy Humphrey and his brother...
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 7, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Brunious
about_quotes.evidence: Then I kind of started traveling with the Preservation Hall band like really about 1987. I had done a few trips with them before that but it’s when the old guy Percy Humphrey really started getting old, I started doing more and more touring with them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Brunious interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: Most of the older guys – I’d see Percy Humphrey and Willie Humphrey. These guys, they would walk down the street, turn at the corner, and go to Preservation Hall.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: George P. Fleming
about_quotes.evidence: he would teach me, he would show me how to do this... I had a goodie at that time and then he would play it down and he'd show me how to play
